mnemonics: Concurrent Models: Think of a busy kitchen (like a Spaghetti Chef) where multiple cooks (threads) work on different parts of the same meal (program) at the same time, sharing ingredients (resources).
keyPoints: Concurrent models allow multiple parts of a program to execute simultaneously, improving efficiency and resource utilization.
Key challenges include managing shared resources, synchronization (e.g., with locks or semaphores), and avoiding deadlocks.
Common models include the actor model, shared state concurrency, and communicating sequential processes (CSP).
commonMistakes: Assuming that more concurrent threads will always lead to better performance, ignoring the overhead of context switching and synchronization.
Introducing race conditions by improperly managing access to shared mutable state, leading to unpredictable results.
